ddls:
CREATE TABLE "network_environments" (
  "env_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each network environment */,
  "env_name" TEXT /* Name of the network environment */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the network environment */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the network environment */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the network environment */,
  "unique_identifier" TEXT /* Unique identifier for the network environment (e.g., MAC address, network ID) */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date when the network environment was created */,
  "last_updated_date" TEXT /* Date of the last update to the network environment */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the network environment (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("env_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "attack_scenarios" (
  "scenario_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each attack scenario */,
  "scenario_name" TEXT /* Name of the attack scenario */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the attack scenario */,
  "attack_type" TEXT /* Type of attack (e.g., DoS, Brute-force, SSH-MITM) */,
  "severity_level" TEXT /* Severity level of the attack (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "mitigation_strategy" TEXT /* Strategy to mitigate the attack */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("scenario_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the performance metric */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the performance metric */,
  "metric_type" TEXT /* Type of metric (e.g., network, CPU, memory) */,
  "unit_of_measurement" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the metric (e.g., Mbps, %, ms) */,
  "critical_threshold" REAL /* Critical threshold value for the metric */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance data entry */,
  "env_id" INTEGER /* ID of the network environment */,
  "scenario_id" INTEGER /* ID of the attack scenario */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* ID of the performance metric */,
  "time" INTEGER /* Time interval of the data entry */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the performance metric */,
  "source_ip" TEXT /* Source IP address of the data entry */,
  "destination_ip" TEXT /* Destination IP address of the data entry */,
  "protocol" TEXT /* Network protocol used (e.g., TCP, UDP, ICMP) */,
  "data_size" INTEGER /* Size of the data entry in bytes */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the data entry (e.g., normal, suspicious) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_data_env_id FOREIGN KEY ("env_id") REFERENCES network_environments ("env_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_data_metric_id FOREIGN KEY ("metric_id") REFERENCES performance_metrics ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_data_scenario_id FOREIGN KEY ("scenario_id") REFERENCES attack_scenarios ("scenario_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., security analyst, network admin, IT manager) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Date of the last login */,
  "last_password_change_date" TEXT /* Date of the last password change */,
  "failed_login_attempts" INTEGER /* Number of failed login attempts */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the user role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the user role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* JSON array of permissions associated with the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the performance data entry being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the data */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the client accessing the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES performance_data ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "env_id" INTEGER /* ID of the network environment */,
  "scenario_id" INTEGER /* ID of the attack scenario */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* ID of the performance metric */,
  "time" INTEGER /* Time interval of the alert */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of the alert (e.g., high CPU usage, network downtime) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the alert (e.g., active, resolved) */,
  "resolution_date" TEXT /* Date when the alert was resolved */,
  "source_ip" TEXT /* Source IP address of the alert */,
  "destination_ip" TEXT /* Destination IP address of the alert */,
  "impact" TEXT /* Impact of the alert (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_env_id FOREIGN KEY ("env_id") REFERENCES network_environments ("env_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_metric_id FOREIGN KEY ("metric_id") REFERENCES performance_metrics ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_scenario_id FOREIGN KEY ("scenario_id") REFERENCES attack_scenarios ("scenario_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alert_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert log entry */,
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* ID of the alert */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user taking the action */,
  "action_type" TEXT /* Type of the action (e.g., acknowledge, resolve) */,
  "action_date" TEXT /* Date when the action was taken */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Comments or notes about the action */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user taking the action */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the client taking the action */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alert_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alert_logs_alert_id FOREIGN KEY ("alert_id") REFERENCES alerts ("alert_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "report_name" TEXT /* Name of the report */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of the report (e.g., performance, security) */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the report */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date the report was created */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path to the report */,
  "last_modified_date" TEXT /* Date of the last modification to the report */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the report (e.g., draft, published) */,
  "file_size" INTEGER /* Size of the report file in bytes */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_report_type FOREIGN KEY ("report_type") REFERENCES report_types ("type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "report_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the report type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the report type */,
  "default_template" TEXT /* Default template for the report type */,
  "example_report" TEXT /* Example report for the report type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "network_policies" (
  "policy_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each network policy */,
  "env_id" INTEGER /* ID of the network environment */,
  "policy_name" TEXT /* Name of the network policy */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the network policy */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the policy */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date the policy was created */,
  "last_updated_date" TEXT /* Date of the last update to the policy */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the policy (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("policy_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_policies_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_policies_env_id FOREIGN KEY ("env_id") REFERENCES network_environments ("env_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "policy_rules" (
  "rule_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each policy rule */,
  "policy_id" INTEGER /* ID of the network policy */,
  "rule_name" TEXT /* Name of the policy rule */,
  "rule_description" TEXT /* Description of the policy rule */,
  "condition" TEXT /* Condition that triggers the rule */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action to be taken when the condition is met */,
  "severity_level" TEXT /* Severity level of the rule (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "priority" INTEGER /* Priority of the rule (e.g., 1, 2, 3) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rule_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_policy_rules_policy_id FOREIGN KEY ("policy_id") REFERENCES network_policies ("policy_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "policy_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each policy log entry */,
  "rule_id" INTEGER /* ID of the policy rule */,
  "trigger_date" TEXT /* Date when the rule was triggered */,
  "action_taken" TEXT /* Action taken based on the rule */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Comments or notes about the action */,
  "source_ip" TEXT /* Source IP address of the log entry */,
  "destination_ip" TEXT /* Destination IP address of the log entry */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the client triggering the rule */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_policy_logs_rule_id FOREIGN KEY ("rule_id") REFERENCES policy_rules ("rule_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_thresholds" (
  "threshold_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance threshold */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* ID of the performance metric */,
  "threshold_value" REAL /* Threshold value for the metric */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert to generate if the threshold is exceeded */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the threshold */,
  "metric_type" TEXT /* Type of metric (e.g., network, CPU, memory) */,
  "unit_of_measurement" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the threshold (e.g., Mbps, %, ms) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("threshold_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_thresholds_metric_id FOREIGN KEY ("metric_id") REFERENCES performance_metrics ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "integration_endpoints" (
  "endpoint_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each integration endpoint */,
  "endpoint_name" TEXT /* Name of the integration endpoint */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL of the integration endpoint */,
  "api_key" TEXT /* API key for authentication */,
  "last_sync_date" TEXT /* Date of the last successful sync */,
  "last_failed_sync_date" TEXT /* Date of the last failed sync */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the endpoint (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("endpoint_id")
)
